topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

The phone buzzed, a jarring interruption in the otherwise quiet afternoon. Leo stared at the screen, his thumb hovering over the keyboard. He’d meant to send a quick reminder to his roommate about the grocery list, but his finger slipped, landing squarely on a contact he hadn't intended to reach. A slow, flat exhale escaped him. He watched the message send, a small, almost imperceptible slump settling into his shoulders. It was just a text, nothing dramatic, just another misplaced keystroke in the endless stream of digital noise. He didn't feel the sting of error, only the mild inconvenience of having to correct it later.

[story 2]

Maya scrolled through her feed, the blue light washing over her face. Another notification popped up—a text from an unknown number. She glanced at the screen, her expression remaining utterly neutral. It was clearly meant for someone else, a rambling anecdote about a weekend trip she had no part in. A faint twitch played at the corner of her mouth, quickly suppressed. She didn't feel any surge of alarm or frustration; it was just another piece of digital debris, something to be acknowledged and then dismissed. She tapped the reply button, typed a brief, noncommittal acknowledgment, and put the phone face down without any real investment in the outcome.

[story 3]

Mark leaned back in his chair, the cheap plastic groaning under his weight. He picked up his phone, intending to check the time, but his gaze snagged on an incoming message. It was a lengthy, overly familiar message addressed to his boss. He read it, a blank canvas of comprehension washing over him. He didn't feel the usual rush of anxiety that accompanied miscommunication. Instead, a heavy sense of detachment settled over him, like dust on a seldom-used surface. He let the notification sit there for a moment longer than necessary, then simply closed the app, returning his focus to the dull hum of the office air conditioning.
